title: Pyrus subrotunda
uses: Pyrus subrotunda has been traditionally used in some cultures for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. Its bark and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Some studies suggest that Pyrus subrotunda may have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects.
taxonomy: Pyrus subrotunda is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as rose or stone fruits. The genus Pyrus is further classified within the order Rosales and the clade eurosids I. The classification of Pyrus subrotunda reflects its close relationships with other pear species. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Pyrus subrotunda shares characteristics with other deciduous trees and shrubs.
benefits: Pyrus subrotunda, also known as the Chinese pear, is a species of pear tree that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The fruit, leaves, and bark of the tree are believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The fruit is said to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of constipation, while the leaves are used to treat fever and cough. The bark is used to treat various ailments, including rheumatism and skin conditions. Additionally, the tree is rich in vitamins and minerals, making it a nutritious addition to a balanced diet.

constituents: Pyrus subrotunda contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. parts: Pyrus subrotunda is native to certain regions in Asia, and the bark of the Pyrus subrotunda tree has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. preparations: Pyrus subrotunda, also known as the small-leaf pear, has been used in traditional herbal preparations for its purported medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and fruits of the plant have been employed in various remedies, although the efficacy and safety of these uses are not well-documented.
